name: POV First-Person
description: Human-POV format. Viewer is projected into the scene from first person via a centered text overlay ("POV: you just…"). The creator plays the character on camera with reactive expressions; the scene develops over 8-25s and lands on a punchline, emotional peak, or cliffhanger. Highest share-rate and save-rate format because of relatability — the viewer recognizes themselves and forwards it. Works across comedy, dating, lifestyle, B2B SaaS ("POV: you're the only marketer at a startup"), retail. Distinct from ai-vegetables (which is POV but vegetable-anthropomorphic) — this is human, broader, character-driven.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — selfie-style or face-cam framing of the POV character. One persona keyframe locks the look; subsequent frames reuse it as a self-ref so the character is consistent across cuts.
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro (default) or google/veo-3.1 via OpenRouter for talking-character animation. 5-8s clips, 1-3 total. Eyes-to-camera, micro-reactions, in-character expression. generate_audio: false (VO is added separately).
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — single character voice, emotional weight matched to the payoff (deadpan / panicked / wistful / fed-up). Optional: skip VO entirely and rely on text overlay + trending sound.
stackSummary.captions: OpenRouter whisper-1 → word-level captions. HormoziCaptions for comedy / B2B (impact); MinimalCaptions or TikTok-white-stroke for lifestyle / dating / emotional. The POV: line is rendered as a separate large centered overlay, not as captions.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music (optional emotional bed) OR a trending / original sound borrowed from the platform reference. POV format leans on platform sound culture more than original score — when the brief cites a trend, the trending sound is mandatory.

constants.structure: 0-2s POV text hook → 2-10s scene develops with reactive expressions → 10-20s payoff (punchline / emotional peak / cliffhanger)
constants.povTextPlacement: centered, big, top-third or middle, persists across the whole hook beat (≥ 2s on screen)

variationAxes: niche (comedy / dating / lifestyle / B2B-SaaS / retail / parenting / fitness)
POV tense (you-are-X / you-just-X / coming-home-to-X / it's-the-year-Y / your-X-is-Y)
payoff style (punchline / emotional peak / cliffhanger / cut-to-black)
shot count (single-shot face-cam vs multi-cut — POV text + reaction + payoff cutaway)
VO presence (full VO / no VO + trending sound only / hybrid)
doNotCopyLiterally: POV scenario line, character look, payoff. The structure is the template; the content is per-project.
referenceNotes: No reference required by default — POV characters are AI-generated personas. If the brief mentions a specific named creator (Brittany Broski, Drew Afualo, etc.) or a real brand, the reference-required gate (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) re-engages: a face / brand reference must exist at workspace/projects/<id>/assets/uploaded/. AI-improvised likeness of a real person is never acceptable.
